headline: 9 core and auxiliary Solana programs discovered across AMM, CLMM, CPMM, LaunchLab, routing, farms, and staking; comprehensive audit trail and multisig governance documented.
short_headline: 9 Solana programs; audits and multisig governance documented

findings.text: Solana explorer search via search query results yielded AMM Program Address 675kPX9MHTjS2zt1qfr1NYHuzeLXfQM9H24wFSUt1Mp8 and token address 4k3Dyjzvzp8eMZWUXbBCjEvwSkkk59S5iCNLY3QrkX6R from Solana explorer transaction history.

Official Raydium security documentation page lists all core program addresses with roles, upgrade authorities, and GitHub links. 9 programs catalogued: AMM v4, CLMM, CPMM, LaunchLab, AMM Routing, Ecosystem Farms, Staking, Farm Staking, and Stable Swap (deprecated).

Raydium audit documentation references 9 audit reports from 2021–2026 covering Order-book AMM, CLMM, Staking, CPMM, Burn & Earn, and LaunchLab. Auditors include Kudelski Security, OtterSec, MadShield, Halborn, and Sec3.

GitHub organization https://github.com/raydium-io hosts multiple canonical repositories: raydium-amm (AMM v4), raydium-clmm (CLMM), raydium-cp-swap (CPMM), raydium-library (CLI and library), raydium-sdk-V2 (TypeScript SDK), and raydium-docs.
Single chain protocol (Solana). All 9 programs deployed on Solana mainnet with no cross-chain deployments.
No factory enumeration attempted; Raydium operates a permissionless pool creation model but does not expose enumeration views in documentation. Pool discovery delegated to future work.
All admin_addresses entries use role vocabulary from controlled taxonomy: 'admin' (treasury multisig), 'proxy_admin' (program upgrade multisig), 'program' (core programs), and 'token'.
All 9 addresses traced to https://docs.raydium.io/raydium/protocol/security and GitHub README. Treasury multisig (3/5) and program upgrade multisig (3/4) documented with Squads V4 links.

rationale.verdict: Discovery of 9 core Solana programs, 2 multisigs, and RAY token. Raydium operates upgradeable programs under 3/4 multisig with 24-hour timelock. Audit trail from 2021–2026; active bug bounty via Immunefi. All core addresses and governance structure sourced from official documentation.

evidence.shows: AMM v4 repository with mainnet program address 675kPX9MHTjS2zt1qfr1NYHuzeLXfQM9H24wFSUt1Mp8 and program deployments table.

Comprehensive security documentation listing 9 programs (AMM v4, CLMM, CPMM, LaunchLab, AMM Routing, Ecosystem Farms, Staking, Farm Staking, Stable Swap deprecated) with addresses, GitHub links, upgrade authorities, and multisig structure (3/4 Squads with 24-hour timelock).
Raydium main documentation page describing protocol as decentralized exchange on Solana with three pool types (CLMM, CPMM, AMM v4), LaunchLab token launch platform, and permissionless features.
Raydium Protocol GitHub organization with 28 repositories including raydium-amm, raydium-clmm, raydium-cp-swap, raydium-library, raydium-sdk-V2, and raydium-docs.
Raydium operates active bug bounty on Immunefi with rewards capped at 10% of economic damage (minimum $50,000 USD). Details scope, vulnerability classification, and payout mechanisms.

unknowns: D6: Factory-discovered child pools for permissionless AMM; cap at 50 children not triggered—Raydium does not document enumeration method for all pools, so child pool discovery deferred.

protocol_metadata.about: Raydium is a decentralized exchange and automated market maker (AMM) built on Solana, launched in February 2021. It enables users to swap tokens, trade perpetuals, and earn yield by providing liquidity across three pool types: AMM v4 (legacy constant-product), CLMM (concentrated liquidity), and CPMM (latest constant-product with multi-fee tiers). The protocol also includes LaunchLab, a permissionless token launch platform using bonding curves, and permissionless farms for liquidity incentives.
